Assembly: The assembly bPhaCar2.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. It was not possible to assemble the W chromosome in this assembly as although the sequence data is from a female the HiC data was from a male.

Assembly: The assembly bAytFul3.1 is based on 55x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z and W chromosomes identified based on copy number in the diploid HIC map. PAR region is identified as 0-~2.33Mb on Chromosomes Z and W. Order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome W from ~3.15-19.0Mb, Chromosome 33 from 0-0.75Mb, Chromosome 35 from ~0.17-0.46Mb, and Chromosome 36 from ~0.10-0.28Mb is unsure.

Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bLusSve1 of the Bluethroat. The sample was provided by Arild Johnsen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bLusSve1 is based on 30x PromethION R10 Oxford Nanopore data and 55x Arima Hi-C data. The R10 ONT reads were assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been added to hap1.

Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the red-backed shrike, Lanius collurio, with a sample provided by Jan T. Lifjeld. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The bLanCol1.1 assembly is based on 32x PacBio HiFi and 38x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1.

Assembly: This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Turdus philomelos (bTurPhi1.1) is based on 45X ONT data and 44X Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with Nextdenovo, polishing with Medaka and Hapo-G, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. Additionally, 1500 regions totaling 394.031 Mb (with the largest being 2.72 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 6 haplotypic regions, totaling 2.5Mb, (with the largest being 0.789Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. The Z sexual chromosome was identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

Assembly: The assembly bAnaPla2.hap1.1 is based on 65x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly of Somateria mollissima, common name common eider, based on a sample provided by Morten Helberg. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly bSomMol1.h2.1 is based on 36 PacBio HiFi data, 52x Arima Hi-C data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. No mitochondrial genomes were found in the data. Both W and Z chromosomes are found in hap1. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly bEriRub2.2 is based on 41x PacBio data, 22x 10X Genomics Chromium data, and 42x Dovetail Hi-C data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: 'Trionyx triunguis was assembled with Hifiasm (version 0.25.0-r726). Contaminations were removed with FCS-GX (version 0.5.5) based on the ALL database released on 2023/01. Remaining haplotypic duplications were identified with purge_dups (version 1.2.6) but to avoid overpurging of repetitive sequences, haplotig duplictions were removed in the manual curation step. Due to the highly contiguous contig assembly, the scaffolding was done in manual curation step as well. The manual curation was done with PretextView (version 1.0.3) and GRIT_Rapid (git: 1a3d79a8). Due to the very high quality of the assembly as well as the HiC sequencing data, more repeat structures could be resolved within the corresponding chromosomes. Though those repeat structures were mainly resolved by the HiC contact map and PacBio read coverage, there might be a risk that the order and/or orientation is not to 100% correct. Here are the coordinates of the chromosome intervals of those regions: SUPER_5 56.3Mb-59.5Mb, SUPER_11 6.1Mb-29.3Mb, SUPER_15 16.4Mb-37.7Mb, SUPER_18 0-2.8Mb, SUPER_26 14.3Mb-16.5Mb'.

Assembly: On Oct 3, 2025 this sequence version replaced OZ065561.2.; The assembly mMyoMys1.hap1.2 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly cbPohNuta1.1 is based on 104x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly appears to suggest that this species is a degraded tetraploid with weak interacting HiC signal between putative homeologous chromosomes. Multiple inversions can be observed between the homeologs, and also large fusion/fission events. Chromosome 15 was split into two homeologs, chromosome 22 and 8. In addition chromosome 8 was fused to a new chromosome and there is also chromosome 21, which seems to not have a homeolog.

Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the European greenfinch, Chloris Chloris, from a sample provided by Jan T. Lifjeld. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The bChlchl1.1 assembly is based on 29x PacBio HiFi and 76x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1.

The assembly process included the following steps: initial ONT assembly generation with NextDeNovo, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Decap, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with 3D-DNA. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 1,524 regions totaling 492 Mb (with the largest being 3.8 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Assembly: The assembly bZosLat1.hap1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Les Christidis' 1990 research on the karyotype of Zosterops lateralis reports a diploid chromosome number of 78. This research is detailed in the book Animal Cytogenetics: Chordata 3B: Aves, published by Gebruder Borntraeger, which examines avian cytogenetics.In our genome assembly, we identified a haploid chromosome count of 39 autosomes plus the Z and W sex chromosomes. It is possible that some chromosome joins are not highlighted by the Hi-C signal or that data for certain joins may be missing, but this cannot be concluded from our assembly.The microchromosomes were identified through second-shot curation of scaffolds smaller than 20 Mbp, combined with homology-based identification using known microchromosome genes via Microfinder.Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly.Chromosomes Z was identified through synteny analysis with Taeniopygia guttata (GCA_003957565.4).The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 23 (1,400 - 7,712 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 31 (1,100 - 5,932 Kbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly mEptNil1.1 is based on 33x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly bCapEur3.1 is based on 87x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Arrow polishing assisted by Merfin, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly bAegCau2.hap1.1 is based on 68x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. MicroFinder was used to order scaffolds prior to curation. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The Z chromosome was identified based on synteny with Zosterops lateralis (GCA_965231275.1).
